key_components: async generate() method with system_prompt, user_prompt, response_schema parameters
file_insights.notes: Core provider abstraction defining interface for all LLM implementations
Proper package structure with explicit exports via __all__

patterns_discovered.pattern: Abstract Base Class (ABC) for Provider Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Used ABC metaclass to define abstract interface for LLM providers
patterns_discovered.benefit: Forces implementation of required methods in concrete subclasses

patterns_discovered.description: generate() is async while get_model_name() is sync
patterns_discovered.rationale: Async for I/O-bound LLM calls, sync for metadata retrieval

patterns_discovered.description: Used 'type[BaseModel] | None' instead of Optional syntax
Explicit __all__ declaration for clean public API
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear interface and prevents accidental internal exposure

gotchas_discovered.note: Intentional design choice but may limit flexibility if model name retrieval becomes async in future
Using 'type[BaseModel] | None' requires Python 3.10+. Older Python versions would fail
Abstract methods have only one-liner docstrings without Args/Returns documentation

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created abstract LLMProvider base class with proper ABC pattern and clear method signatures as per specification. Package structure properly initialized with exports.

recommendations.recommendation: Add Python version constraint to project config
recommendations.rationale: Code uses Python 3.10+ union type syntax; should enforce minimum Python version

recommendations.recommendation: Expand docstrings with Args and Returns sections
recommendations.rationale: Would improve IDE autocomplete and API documentation clarity

recommendations.recommendation: Consider consistency between async generate() and sync get_model_name()
recommendations.rationale: If model name will remain always synchronous, add comment explaining design rationale
Could validate that response_schema is a proper Pydantic model before use in implementations
